cards.definition: The typical planting spacing range for Arabica Tall varieties, balancing canopy space against planting density.
cards.analogy: Room enough for each plant's mature canopy to develop without immediately competing with its neighbor.
cards.why_it_matters: Spacing tighter or looser than this range signals either an unusually high-density block or wasted land.
cards.cloze: Arabica Tall varieties are typically planted at a spacing of ___ feet.

cards.definition: The typical planting spacing for Tall Robusta, wider than Arabica Tall's to match Robusta's larger mature canopy.
cards.analogy: A wider footprint reserved for a bigger mature plant, the same logic as spacing large fruit trees further apart than shrubs.
cards.why_it_matters: Applying Arabica spacing to a Robusta block would crowd the canopy well before maturity.
cards.cloze: Tall Robusta plants are typically spaced at ___ feet.

cards.definition: The standard size for a coffee planting pit — cubic, not just a shallow scoop.
cards.analogy: A pit sized to give young roots real room to establish, not just a token hole to drop a seedling into.
cards.why_it_matters: A pit dug smaller than this is a common, invisible cause of poor early establishment.
cards.cloze: Standard coffee planting pits measure ___ feet in length, breadth, and depth.

cards.definition: How long a freshly dug planting pit should be left open to the elements before being filled and planted.
cards.analogy: Letting dug soil air out and break down before it goes back in, the way turned compost needs time before use.
cards.why_it_matters: Filling and planting a pit immediately after digging skips a step that helps soil structure and pest exposure.
cards.cloze: After opening, planting pits are exposed for weathering for ___ days before filling.

cards.definition: The typical age of a coffee seedling (as a root plant) when it's ready to move from nursery to field, timed to the June-July monsoon window.
cards.analogy: A young plant old enough to establish on its own, moved at the point in the calendar with the best odds of survival.
cards.why_it_matters: A seedling planted much younger or older than this window is taking on avoidable transplant risk.
cards.cloze: Coffee seedlings are typically field-planted at ___ months old (root plants) during June-July.

cards.definition: The dose of thimmet (forate) crystals added to each planting pit as a preventive measure against cockchafer grub damage.
cards.analogy: A small preventive dose placed exactly where the pest would attack, not a broadcast treatment.
cards.why_it_matters: A cheap, targeted step against a specific early-establishment risk, easy to skip and easy to verify was done.
cards.cloze: At planting, ___ grams of thimmet/forate crystals are added per pit to avoid cockchafer problems.
